oligos.name: Universal Primer oSP737
oligos.role: Universal forward primer for site-directed mutagenesis cloning of sgRNA spacers into pSP618 (primes the U6/backbone side)
oligos.notes: 100uM stock; used with the sgRNA-specific SDM primer to amplify pSP618 during whole-vector SDM cloning.

oligos.name: sgRNA-specific SDM primer
oligos.role: Site-directed mutagenesis primer that inserts an individual sgRNA spacer; 3' portion anneals to the sgRNA scaffold constant region

components.name: sgRNA spacer
components.role: 20-nt guide spacer of interest (replaces the poly-N region)

components.name: sgRNA scaffold (constant region cr1)
components.role: start of the sgRNA constant scaffold; anneals to pSP618 backbone

oligos.name: Sanger sequencing primer oSP652
oligos.role: Sanger sequencing primer to verify the cloned sgRNA spacer

oligos.name: sgRNA-specific Reverse oligo oSP1735
oligos.role: sgRNA-backbone-specific reverse primer spiked into the 10x GEM master mix to exponentially amplify the sgRNA fragment during in-GEM amplification
oligos.notes: 1.2 uL of 50 uM added to the 10x scATAC Master Mix. Acts as the Reverse oligo opposite the 10x barcoded Read1-complementary Forward oligo.

oligos.name: 10x barcoded Forward oligo (gel bead scATAC oligo)
oligos.role: Gel-bead oligo complementary to the pre-integrated Nextera Read1 adapter; primes all ATAC (and sgRNA) fragments, adding P5 and the 10x cell barcode

components.name: 10x cell barcode
components.role: 16-nt droplet/cell barcode from the gel bead

components.name: Nextera Read 1 adapter
components.role: Nextera/Tn5 Read1 (mosaic-end) sequence complementary to the pre-integrated sgRNA Read1 adapter

oligos.notes: Inferred 10x Genomics scATAC-seq v1 (PN-1000110) gel-bead oligo architecture; the paper states the bead carries barcoded Forward oligos complementary to the Nextera Read1 adapter. Nextera Read1 mosaic-end sequence is the canonical Tn5 adapter (named in source; sequence is standard).

oligos.name: Pre-integrated Nextera Read1 adapter (lentiviral sgRNA)
oligos.role: 34-bp Nextera Read1 adapter cloned 3' of the sgRNA scaffold in pSP618 so the sgRNA amplifies with ATAC primers
oligos.notes: Named as a '34bp Nextera Read1 adapter' in the source; exact sequence not printed. Canonical Nextera Read1 (Tn5) adapter shown.

oligos.name: Pre-integrated Nextera Read2 adapter (lentiviral sgRNA)
oligos.role: 34-bp Nextera Read2 adapter cloned into a flexible region of the mouse U6 promoter (in place of the loxP site), 5' of the sgRNA spacer
oligos.notes: Named as a '34bp Nextera Read2 adapter' in the source; exact sequence not printed. Canonical Nextera Read2 (Tn5) adapter shown. Placed in the U6 promoter to shorten the sgRNA fragment (internal fragment 214 bp; total sgRNA fragment 362 bp).

oligos.name: sgRNA scaffold / constant region cr1
oligos.role: Constant sgRNA scaffold (Perturb-seq cr1) between spacer and Nextera Read1 adapter; binding site for the oMCB1672 Read1 sequencing primer
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of oMCB1672; the scaffold is read (in reverse orientation) by oMCB1672 to directly sequence the adjacent spacer.

oligos.name: Bio-oSP2053 (biotinylated sgRNA-specific primer)
oligos.role: 5' biotinylated, sgRNA-specific primer with TruSeq Read2 adapter; used for PCR1 linear amplification / biotin-tagging of sgRNA fragments out of the scATAC library

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 adapter
components.role: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 sequence appended to the sgRNA fragment

components.name: sgRNA-specific (U6/backbone)
components.role: 3' primer arm that anneals to the U6/backbone region adjacent to the spacer

oligos.notes: 5' biotin. Run 25 cycles of linear (single-primer) amplification; enables streptavidin enrichment of sgRNA fragments.

oligos.name: oSP1594 (P5 primer)
oligos.role: P5-specific forward primer for PCR2 exponential amplification of the targeted sgRNA amplicon
oligos.notes: Truncated P5 (matches the P5 already on the scATAC library).

oligos.name: Indexed P7 primer
oligos.role: P7 + i7 sample-index reverse primer for PCR2; anneals to the TruSeq Read2 sequence added by oSP2053

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 (partial)
components.role: anneals to the TruSeq Read2 sequence introduced by oSP2053

oligos.name: Custom Read1 sequencing primer oMCB1672
oligos.role: Custom Read1 sequencing primer that anneals to the sgRNA scaffold and reads directly into the sgRNA spacer
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of the sgRNA scaffold cr1; gives a short spacer read (vs ~150 cycles if sequencing from the Nextera Read1 adapter).

final_library.source_label: Spear-ATAC targeted sgRNA amplicon (final sequenceable library; sgRNA spacer + 10x cell barcode)
annotation_lines: GCCACTTTTTCAAGTTGATAACGGACTAGCCTTATTTAAACTTGCTATGCTGTTTCCAGCTTAGCTCTTAAAC = sgRNA scaffold / constant region cr1 (oMCB1672 Read 1 binding site)
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC = pre-integrated Nextera Read 2 adapter (reverse complement, embedded in U6)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC = TruSeq Read 2 adapter (appended by oSP2053)

evidence.locator: Spear-ATAC targeted sgRNA amplicon (final sequenceable library; sgRNA spacer + 10x cell barcode)

library_generation.title: Clone sgRNA spacer into pSP618 (Spear-ATAC backbone)
library_generation.summary: An individual guide spacer is inserted by site-directed mutagenesis into pSP618, whose U6-sgRNA cassette is pre-flanked with Nextera Read2 (in the U6 promoter) and Nextera Read1 (3' of the scaffold) adapters.
library_generation.note: pSP618 (derived from pMJ114; Addgene, GenBank MW852482) carries a mouse U6 promoter with a 34 bp Nextera Read2 adapter in place of the loxP site, the Perturb-seq constant region cr1, and a 34 bp Nextera Read1 adapter. Q5 whole-vector SDM with oSP737 + the sgRNA-specific primer, then KLD (kinase/ligase/DpnI).
library_generation.product: 5'- [U6 promoter]--[Nextera Read2 adapter]--[SGRNA_SPACER:20]--[scaffold cr1: GTTTAAGAGCTAAGCTGGAAAC...GTGGC]--[Nextera Read1 adapter] -3'

library_generation.title: Lentiviral transduction + FACS sort
library_generation.summary: Pooled sgRNA lentivirus is transduced at low MOI (<0.05) and sgRNA+ (BFP+) cells are FACS-sorted, giving each cell a single genome-integrated, adapter-flanked sgRNA cassette.
library_generation.note: dCas9-KRAB cell lines (K562/GM12878/MCF7). Low MOI minimizes double transduction so each cell carries one guide.
library_generation.product: genomic DNA: 5'- ...--[Nextera Read2]--[SGRNA_SPACER:20]--[scaffold cr1]--[Nextera Read1]--... -3'   (single integrant per cell)

library_generation.title: Nuclei isolation & Tn5 transposition (scATAC)
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are isolated and hyperactive Tn5 transposes open chromatin, inserting Nextera adapters into accessible genomic DNA; the sgRNA cassette is already adapter-flanked.
library_generation.note: Standard 10x scATAC transposition; the pre-integrated adapters mean the sgRNA fragment does not depend on nearby transposition.
library_generation.product:
ATAC fragment: 5'- [Nextera R1 ME][open-chromatin insert][Nextera R2 ME] -3'
sgRNA cassette (already flanked): 5'- [Nextera R2]--[SGRNA_SPACER:20]--[scaffold cr1]--[Nextera R1] -3'

library_generation.title: GEM generation & in-GEM amplification (15 cycles)
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are encapsulated on the 10x Controller; a bead Forward oligo (P5 + cell barcode, complementary to Nextera Read1) primes all fragments while spiked-in oSP1735 exponentially amplifies the sgRNA fragment, tagging both with the cell barcode.
library_generation.note: In-GEM linear amplification extended from 12 to 15 cycles (adds ~3 rounds of exponential sgRNA amplification). Forward oligo = P5-[CELL_BARCODE:16]-Nextera Read1; Reverse oligo = oSP1735.

library_generation.title: Clean-ups + PCR to attach P7 / sample index (scATAC library)
library_generation.summary: After DynaBead/SPRI clean-ups, exponential PCR attaches the P7 flow-cell adapter and an i7 sample index, completing the standard 10x scATAC-seq library (which contains the barcoded sgRNA fragments).
library_generation.note: This is the finished ~150 nM scATAC-seq library; sgRNA fragments carry P5/cell barcode on the Read1 side and P7/i7 on the Read2 side.
library_generation.product: 5'- P5[CELL_BARCODE:16][Nextera R1]--[scaffold cr1]--[SGRNA_SPACER:20]--[U6/Nextera R2 region]--[i7 index]--P7 -3'   (final scATAC library)

library_generation.title: PCR1 — biotinylated linear amplification (targeted sgRNA, 25 cycles)
library_generation.summary: 2.5 uL of scATAC library is split across 8 reactions and linearly amplified with the 5'-biotinylated sgRNA-specific primer oSP2053, appending a TruSeq Read2 adapter and biotin-tagging sgRNA strands.
library_generation.note: Single-primer (linear) amplification enriches sgRNA fragments before selection; 2X NEBNext, 25 cycles, MinElute clean-up.

library_generation.title: Streptavidin enrichment / scATAC disenrichment
library_generation.summary: Biotinylated sgRNA strands are captured on Streptavidin MyOne C1 beads and washed, disenriching contaminating scATAC fragments.
library_generation.note: B&W buffer washes; beads resuspended in 40 uL ddH2O as PCR2 input. Biotin selection sharply increases targeted-amplification specificity.

library_generation.title: PCR2 — attach P5 / P7+index (targeted sgRNA amplicon, 15 cycles)
library_generation.summary: Bead-bound sgRNA fragments are exponentially amplified with oSP1594 (P5) and the indexed P7 primer (which anneals to the TruSeq Read2 added in PCR1), yielding the final targeted sgRNA sequencing library.
library_generation.note: 8x100 uL reactions, 15 cycles; supernatant collected after magnet, MinElute clean-up, KAPA quant. Product = final_library.

library_sequencing.primer: oMCB1672 (custom sgRNA Read1 primer, sequence = sgRNA scaffold cr1)
library_sequencing.note: oMCB1672 is identical to the top-strand scaffold cr1, so it anneals to the bottom strand and extends rightward directly into the 20-nt sgRNA spacer (perturbation identity). Sequencing from the Nextera Read1 adapter instead would require a much longer, more expensive ~150-cycle read.
library_sequencing.diagram:
oMCB1672 (Read1 primer, = scaffold cr1)
                     5'-GCCACTTTTT..CTTAGCTCTTAAAC------------------>  (reads spacer)
5'- ...TGTATAAGAGACAGGCCACTTTTT..CTTAGCTCTTAAACNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNCTGTCTCTTATACAC... -3'
3'- ...ACATATTCTCTGTCCGGTGAAAAA..GAATCGAGAATTTGNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNGACAGAGAATATGTG... -5'
     [Nextera R1]     [------ sgRNA scaffold cr1 ------]|<-- spacer 20 nt -->|[Nextera R2rc]

library_sequencing.primer: Index 1 sequencing primer (binds TruSeq Read 2 adapter)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 8-nt sample index adjacent to P7 that identifies the Spear-ATAC sample (up to 8 samples per 10x Controller run).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 1 (i7) read primer -->
                                   binds TruSeq Read 2, reads 8-nt index
5'- ...GGGATACTTAGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCACNNNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- ...CCCTATGAATCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTGNNNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
       [U6]      [------ TruSeq Read 2 adapter ------]|i7 idx|[----- P7 rc -----]

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 16-nt 10x cell barcode that links each sgRNA spacer back to the matching scATAC chromatin-accessibility profile of the same nucleus. Primer = Nextera Read1 (top-strand sequence), anneals to the bottom strand and extends toward P5 across the barcode.
library_sequencing.diagram:
<-------TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3'
                                 (i5 cell-barcode read primer = Nextera Read 1)
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNTCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG... -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTGNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNAGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC... -5'
    [--------- P5 ---------]|<-- cell barcode 16 nt -->|[------ Nextera Read 1 ------]

title: Spear-ATAC
description: Spear-ATAC (Single-cell perturbations with an accessibility read-out using scATAC-seq) is a droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq CRISPRi screen. Cells are transduced at low MOI with a lentiviral sgRNA whose spacer is pre-flanked by Nextera Read1/Read2 adapters, then processed on the 10x Genomics scATAC-seq platform. In-GEM linear amplification tags open-chromatin fragments with a 10x cell barcode via a Read1-complementary forward oligo, while a spiked-in sgRNA-specific reverse primer (oSP1735) exponentially amplifies the integrated sgRNA fragment so the sgRNA spacer is read out directly from genomic DNA. The scATAC reads report chromatin accessibility; a separate biotin-enriched targeted amplicon reads the sgRNA spacer (perturbation identity) paired to the same cell barcode.

original_publication.title: High-throughput single-cell chromatin accessibility CRISPR screens enable unbiased identification of regulatory networks in cancer

authors.name: Sarah E. Pierce
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics, Stanford University School of Medicine, Stanford, CA, USA

authors.name: Jeffrey M. Granja
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics and Center for Personal and Dynamic Regulomes, Stanford University School of Medicine, Stanford, CA, USA

authors.name: William J. Greenleaf
authors.affiliation: Department of Genetics; Center for Personal and Dynamic Regulomes, Stanford University School of Medicine, Stanford, CA; Chan Zuckerberg Biohub, San Francisco, CA, USA

throughput.summary: Spear-ATAC profiled 104,592 cells representing 414 sgRNA knock-down populations. One 7-minute 10x Controller run processes up to 80,000 nuclei (up to 10,000 cells x 8 samples); ~35-100x higher throughput and ~20x lower cost than Perturb-ATAC (~$0.46/cell vs ~$9.80/cell).
throughput.cells: 104,592 total; pilot run captured 6,390 nuclei (48% assigned to sgRNA, n=3,045 at >80% specificity)
throughput.dna: Paired scATAC-seq chromatin accessibility fragments + integrated sgRNA spacer fragments read from genomic DNA (no RNA readout)

publication.statistical_model: chromVAR background-corrected motif-accessibility deviation (Z-score) scores for TF activity; differential accessibility by binomial test (pilot/time-course, getMarkerFeatures) and Wilcoxon test (large screens); sgRNA assignment thresholded at >=20 sgRNA counts and >=0.8 specificity to the top target.

other.label: sgRNA readout
other.value: Reads sgRNA spacer directly from genomic DNA (not RNA), unlike Perturb-ATAC/CRISPR-sciATAC

other.label: Plasmid
other.value: pSP618 lentiviral backbone (from pMJ114); Addgene, GenBank accession MW852482

other.label: sgRNA detection gain
other.value: ~4-fold from pre-integrated Nextera adapters; ~40-fold overall vs lentiviral integration + traditional droplet scATAC

other.label: Kit
other.value: 10x Genomics scATAC-seq kit v1 (PN-1000110); analysis with Cell Ranger ATAC 1.2.0 and ArchR 0.9.5 on hg38

data_processing.summary: Two readouts are analyzed in parallel. scATAC fragments are demultiplexed and aligned to hg38 with Cell Ranger ATAC, then processed in ArchR (tile + gene-score matrices, LSI, clustering, UMAP, chromVAR motif deviations); separately the targeted sgRNA amplicon (Read1 = spacer, Index1 = cell barcode) is aligned in R against sgRNA-spacer and cell-barcode whitelists to build a cell-by-sgRNA matrix and assign one sgRNA per cell (>=20 counts and >=0.8 specificity to the top target), and the two are joined on the shared 10x cell barcode.
data_processing.statistical_model: chromVAR background-corrected motif-deviation Z-scores; differential accessibility by binomial test (getMarkerFeatures, pilot/time-course) and Wilcoxon test (large screens); sgRNA assignment thresholded at >=20 counts and >=0.8 specificity to the top target.
